subject: Math

description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to multiply and divide rational numbers.

description_plain: Your child understands that subtracting a rational number is the same as adding its opposite.

description: Describe situations and show that a number and its opposite have a sum of 0 (additive inverses).

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to add and subtract rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to add and subtract rational numbers by building on what they already know about numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations to add, subtract, multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities.

description: Compute unit rates, including those that involve complex fractions, with like or different units.

description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Reading and constructing maps

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Describe the functional relationship between two quantities from a graph or a verbal description.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two quantities are related by looking at a graph or listening to a description.

subject: Math
description: Determine the x-intercept of a linear function.
description_plain: Your child finds where a line crosses the x-axis on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Determine the parameters of a linear function.
description_plain: Your child identifies the parts of a linear function, like slope and intercept.

subject: Math
description: Explain the parameters of a linear function based on the context of a problem.
description_plain: Your child explains what the parts of a linear function mean in a real-world problem.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model linear relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child uses functions to show how two quantities change together in a straight-line relationship.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child uses functions to show how different amounts are connected.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of nonlinear functions.
description_plain: Your child gives examples of functions that do not make straight lines on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the graph of a linear function has a constant rate of change.
description_plain: Your child understands that a straight-line graph shows a constant rate of change.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function, whose parameters are the slope (m) and the y-intercept (b).
description_plain: Your child understands the equation y = mx + b means a line where m is the slope and b is where it crosses the y-axis.

subject: Math
description: Investigate the differences between linear and nonlinear functions.
description_plain: Your child explores how linear functions are different from nonlinear functions.

subject: Math
description: Compare characteristics of two functions each represented in a different way.
description_plain: Your child compares two functions shown in different ways, like graphs or tables.

subject: Math
description: Graph a function.
description_plain: Graph a function.

subject: Math
description: Determine if a relation is a function.
description_plain: Your child decides if a set of pairs is a function by checking if each input has only one output.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function assigns to each input exactly one output.
description_plain: Your child understands that a function gives exactly one output for each input.

subject: Math
description: Explore the concept of functions.
description_plain: Your child learns what functions are and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate and compare functions.
description_plain: Your child defines, evaluates, and compares different functions.

subject: Math
description: Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible association between the two variables.
description_plain: Your child uses relative frequencies in tables to describe how two variables might be related.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects.
description_plain: Your child makes and reads two-way tables that show data about two categories from the same group.

subject: Math
description: Understand the patterns of association in bivariate categorical data displayed in a two-way table.
description_plain: Your child understands how two categories of data can be related by looking at two-way tables.

description_plain: Your child uses parts of a linear model to solve problems with two related measurements.

subject: Math
description: Generate and use a trend line for bivariate data, and informally assess the fit of the line.
description_plain: Your child creates a trend line for two sets of data and checks how well it fits.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots of bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child makes and reads scatter plots to find patterns between two quantities.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
description_plain: Your child can find and explore patterns between two sets of data.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concepts of volume and find the volume of pyramids, cones and spheres.
description_plain: Your child understands volume and finds the volume of pyramids, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of surface area and find surface area of pyramids.
description_plain: Your child understands surface area and finds the surface area of pyramids.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving surface area and volume.
description_plain: Your child solves problems involving surface area and volume of 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving volume of cones, pyramids and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems to find the volume of cones, pyramids, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between points in a Cartesian coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between points on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in problems in two-and three-dimensional contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles in both flat and 3D problems.

subject: Math
description: Use models to demonstrate a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show why the Pythagorean Theorem works and also explain its reverse.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.
description_plain: Your child understands and can use the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the properties of similar figures to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using the properties of shapes that look the same but are different sizes.

subject: Math
description: Construct and explore the angles created when parallel lines are cut by a transversal.
description_plain: Your child can draw and study the angles formed when parallel lines are crossed by another line.

subject: Math
description: Explore the relationship between the interior and exterior angles of a triangle.
description_plain: Your child can explore how the inside and outside angles of a triangle are related.

subject: Math
description: Derive the sum of the interior angles of a triangle.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the total measure of the inside angles of a triangle.

subject: Math
description: Explore angle relationships and establish informal arguments.
description_plain: Your child can explore how angles relate to each other and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Describe a possible sequence of transformations between two similar figures.
description_plain: Your child can describe steps to change one similar shape into another using movements and resizing.

subject: Math
description: Understand that two-dimensional figures are similar if a series of transformations (rotations, reflections, translations and dilations) can be performed to map the pre-image to the image.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are similar if you can rotate, flip, slide, or resize one to match the other.

subject: Math
description: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations and reflections on two-dimensional figures using coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can describe what happens to a shape on a graph when it is moved, turned, flipped, or resized.

subject: Math
description: Describe a possible sequence of rigid transformations between two congruent figures.
description_plain: Your child can describe steps to move one shape onto another identical shape using only rotations, flips, or slides.

subject: Math
description: Understand that two-dimensional figures are congruent if a series of rigid transformations can be performed to map the preimage to the image.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are exactly the same if you can move one to match the other without resizing.

subject: Math
description: Investigate if orientation is preserved under rigid transformations.
description_plain: Your child investigates whether the direction of a shape stays the same after moving it.

subject: Math
description: Verify that angle measure, betweeness, collinearity and distance are preserved under rigid transformations.
description_plain: Your child checks that angle size, order of points, and distances stay the same when shapes are moved without resizing.

subject: Math
description: Verify experimentally the congruence properties of rigid transformations.
description_plain: Your child tests and confirms that moving shapes without resizing keeps their size and shape the same.

subject: Math
description: Understand congruence and similarity using physical models, transparencies or geometry software.
description_plain: Your child learns about shapes being the same or similar using physical models, overlays, or computer tools.

subject: Math
description: Solve systems of two linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with two linear equations at the same time.

subject: Math
description: Explain why systems of linear equations can have one solution, no solution or infinitely many solutions.
description_plain: Your child can explain why two linear equations can have one solution, no solutions, or many solutions.

subject: Math
description: Explain why solution(s) to a system of two linear equations in two variables correspond to point(s) of intersection of the graphs.
description_plain: Your child understands that solutions to two linear equations are where their graphs cross.

subject: Math
description: Graph systems of linear equations and recognize the intersection as the solution to the system.
description_plain: Your child can graph two linear equations and find where their lines cross to solve the system.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve systems of linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve problems involving two linear equations together.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations and inequalities with rational number coefficients, including equations and inequalities whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and combining like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations and inequalities with fractions and decimals, including those needing distribution and combining like terms.

subject: Math
description: Create and identify linear equations with one solution, infinitely many solutions or no solutions.
description_plain: Your child can create and recognize linear equations that have one solution, no solutions, or many solutions.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations and inequalities with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve linear equations and inequalities and pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve linear equations, inequalities, and pairs of linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Derive the equation y = mx for a line through the origin and the equation y = mx + b for a line intercepting the vertical axis at b.
description_plain: Your child can write the equation of a line through the origin as y = mx and for other lines as y = mx + b.

subject: Math
description: Explain why the slope (m) is the same between any two distinct points on a non-vertical line in the Cartesian coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the slope is the same between any two points on a straight, non-vertical line.

subject: Math
description: Apply concepts of slope and y-intercept to graphs, equations and proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can use slope and y-intercept to understand graphs, equations, and proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Compare two different proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can compare two different proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the unit rate as the slope of the graph.
description_plain: Your child understands that the unit rate is the same as the slope on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Graph proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can graph proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Understand the connections between proportional relationships, lines and linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how proportional relationships connect to lines and linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Use scientific notation and choose units of appropriate size for measurements of very large or very small quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use scientific notation and pick the right units to measure very big or very small things.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation, including problems where both decimal and scientific notation are used.
description_plain: Your child can do math with numbers written in scientific notation and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Use scientific notation to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use scientific notation to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Express very large and very small quantities in scientific notation and approximate how many times larger one is than the other.
description_plain: Your child can write very big or very small numbers in scientific notation and estimate how much bigger one is than the other.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that square roots of non-perfect squares are irrational.
description_plain: Your child understands that square roots of numbers that aren’t perfect squares are irrational (cannot be written as simple fractions).

subject: Math
description: Evaluate square roots of perfect squares less than or equal to 625 and cube roots of perfect cubes less than or equal to 1000.
description_plain: Your child can find square roots of perfect squares up to 625 and cube roots of perfect cubes up to 1000.

subject: Math
description: Solve equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p, where p is a positive rational number.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations like x² = p and x³ = p when p is a positive number.

subject: Math
description: Investigate concepts of square and cube roots.
description_plain: Your child can explore and understand what square roots and cube roots mean.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the properties of integer exponents to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use the rules for exponents with whole numbers to rewrite math expressions in different but equal ways.

subject: Math
description: Work with radicals and integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child works with roots and whole number exponents to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Estimate the value and compare the size of irrational numbers and approximate their locations on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can guess the size of numbers that can't be written as simple fractions and show where they belong on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent representations of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write the same rational number in different forms.

subject: Math
description: Convert decimals which repeat into fractions and fractions into repeating decimals.
description_plain: Your child can change repeating decimals into fractions and fractions into repeating decimals.

subject: Math
description: Understand that all rational numbers have a decimal expansion that terminates or repeats.
description_plain: Your child understands that all rational numbers have decimals that either stop or repeat.

subject: Math
description: Know the differences between rational and irrational numbers.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between numbers that can be written as fractions and those that cannot.

subject: Math
description: Explore the real number system.
description_plain: Your child explores different types of numbers on the number line, including rational and irrational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Know that there are numbers that are not rational, and approximate them by rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child knows there are numbers that can't be written as fractions and can estimate them using fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Draw conclusions from object graphs, picture graphs, T-charts and tallies.
description_plain: Your child can look at simple graphs and charts and explain what they show.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize and represent data with up to three categories.
description_plain: Your child can gather information, sort it into up to three groups, and show it using charts or graphs.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Know the value of a penny, nickel, dime and quarter.
description_plain: Your child knows the value of pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.
description_plain: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate the ability to measure length or distance using objects.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long or far something is by using objects as measuring tools.

subject: Math
description: Compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can compare the lengths of two things by using a third object to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Order three or more objects by length.
description_plain: Your child can put three or more objects in order from shortest to longest or longest to shortest.

subject: Math
description: Measure lengths in non-standard units.
description_plain: Your child measures lengths using everyday objects instead of rulers.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two or four equal shares, and describe the shares and the wholes verbally.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and talk about the parts and the whole shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize two-and three-dimensional shapes from different perspectives and orientations.
description_plain: Your child can recognize flat and solid shapes even when they look different or are turned in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose two-and three-dimensional shapes to build an understanding of part-whole relationships and the properties of the original and composite shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put shapes together and take them apart to understand how parts make up the whole shape.
